description: Only open an absolute http:// or https:// URL in the persistent visible in-app browser session. Defaults to mobile browser mode, using the app WebView's normal mobile profile. Reuses the current page when the URL and browser mode already match unless force_reload is true. Never use this for file:// URLs, local filesystem paths, workspace paths, sandbox paths, generated HTML files, or files you just created; use file tools or the generated attachment instead.
name: browser_open

force_reload.description: Reload even when the current URL already matches.

mode.description: Browser rendering profile. Defaults to mobile. Use desktop only when the user asks for a desktop page or a mobile page is blocked/limited.

url.description: Absolute http:// or https:// URL to open. Do not pass file:// URLs, local paths, workspace paths, sandbox paths, generated HTML files, or files you just created.

description: Read the current visible browser page state. Returns browser_mode, user_agent, page_state, viewport_map, page_change_token, backend_capabilities, optional screenshot metadata, and last_action_effect. Use viewport_map when DOM text is incomplete: it contains visible text blocks, clickable element positions, overlays, bbox, center points, nearby text, action hints, and diagnostics. If screenshot metadata is present and image_analyze is available, analyze the screenshot for visual understanding; otherwise use the JSON state and do not claim visual inspection.
name: browser_snapshot

screenshot_mode.description: Optional screenshot capture preference. Defaults to auto. Screenshots are an optional visual aid; the JSON viewport_map is the non-visual fallback.

description: Click an element in the current browser page. Prefer element_id from the latest browser_snapshot page_state. Legacy index, CSS selector, visible text, and accessibility label are still accepted; text/label clicks may use a visible text ancestor fallback for dynamic JavaScript components. For high-confidence viewport_map targets, click_point may be used as a coordinate fallback. The host verifies the click effect and may perform one safe recovery retry; failures include structured failure_code values such as no_effect_after_click, obscured, site_restricted, login_required, and target_unstable.
name: browser_click

click_point.description: High-confidence viewport coordinate fallback from viewport_map center/clickable_point. Prefer element_id when possible.

element_id.description: Stable element id from browser_snapshot page_state.elements. Preferred target.

index.description: Legacy element index from browser_snapshot interactive elements.

label.description: Accessible label, aria-label, placeholder, or title for the element to click.

selector.description: CSS selector for the element to click.

text.description: Visible text contained by the element to click.

description: Type text into an input or editable element in the current browser page. Prefer element_id from the latest browser_snapshot page_state.
name: browser_type

clear_first.description: Clear existing field value before typing. Defaults to true.

element_id.description: Stable element id from browser_snapshot page_state.elements. Preferred target.

index.description: Legacy element index from browser_snapshot interactive elements.

label.description: Accessible label, aria-label, placeholder, or title for the editable element.

selector.description: CSS selector for the editable element.

submit.description: Submit/press Enter after typing. Requires approval for high-risk flows.

amount.description: Approximate scroll amount in pixels. Defaults to 700.

description: Wait for the browser page to load, settle, or contain expected text. When scroll_to_text is true, the host may scroll the text into view.
name: browser_wait

milliseconds.description: Maximum wait duration in milliseconds. Defaults to 1000.

scroll_to_text.description: If text is provided, scroll the first matching text into view when possible.

text.description: Optional visible text to wait for.

description: Find visible text on the current page and scroll the first match into view.
name: browser_find_text

text.description: Text to find and bring into view.

description: Send simple keyboard keys to the focused browser element. Supported keys are Enter, Escape, Tab, ArrowUp, ArrowDown, ArrowLeft, and ArrowRight.
name: browser_keys

keys.description: Key name or plus-separated key sequence such as Enter, Escape, Tab, ArrowDown.

description: Navigate the persistent browser session back if possible.
name: browser_back

description: Close or clear the persistent browser session. Hiding the UI does not call this tool.
name: browser_close

clear_storage.description: Clear browser cookies and local storage for this app WebView session.
